title: Fix default path for auth.json
body:
Default path should be ${XDG_RUNTIME_DIR}/containers/auth.json according
to the docs.

This regressed to ${XDG_RUNTIME_DIR}/auth.json in #3760.

Fixes: #4227

Signed-off-by: Hunor Csomortáni <csomh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
Hi @csomh. Thanks for your PR.

I'm waiting for a [containers](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) or [openshift](https://github.com/orgs/openshift/people) member to verify that this patch is reasonable to test. If it is, they should reply with `/ok-to-test` on its own line. Until that is done, I will not automatically test new commits in this PR, but the usual testing commands by org members will still work. Regular contributors should [join the org](https://github.com/orgs/openshift/people) to skip this step.

Once the patch is verified, the new status will be reflected by the `ok-to-test` label.

I understand the commands that are listed [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod).

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>

My concern would be losing existing credentials after this change is applied (we won't look at the old default path, so users will have to log in again)... though, given this only happens after an RPM upgrade, I don't suppose that's the worst thing.

/approve

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *csomh*, *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

LGTM assuming happy tests.
Probably worth making a release note for this @mheon once the kit is created.
Failures seem to be flakes, related to #4167 . Is there a way I can re-trigger CI?

@csomh I think you can click on the "details" link next to a failing test and then click on "retest" on the next page.  If not, an admin certainly can and will.  Looks like the tests are cranking ATM, fingers crossed.

I think Podman should not try to hard-code the default path and rely on the defaults implemented in containers/image (see https://github.com/containers/image/blob/master/pkg/docker/config/config.go#L171). @mtrmac, what do you think of making `getPathToAuth(...)` public? We could then drop the function in libpod and use/display what c/image provides.

- I agree Podman should not carry a duplicate of the default logic _in the cases it uses the default_ (in particular, not rootless… or maybe c/image should become rootless-aware in all its defaults, but that would be a bigger project)
- Just exporting `getPathToAuth` would not be sufficient, because the primary reason Podman overrides it (I think) is handling `rootless.GetRootlessUID()`, which c/image does not do (rootless-aware, again)
- `getPathToAuth` defaults to `/run/containers/`_$UID_`/auth.json`, which Podman does not do, but more importantly which does not actually work for non-root UIDs well (the caller is not allowed to modify files in there), so porting this default to Podman seems not quite warranted. [FWIW the Podman’s attempt to create `/run/user/`_$UID_ will fail for the same reason.]
- Podman has two more locations where it tries to create the files. _Assuming_ they are there for a good reason, just using `getPathToAuth` would break those cases.

The quick 60% improvement is for Podman to not set `SystemContext.AuthFilePath` if `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR` is set; that immediately does the right thing for all non-root users, and does not change Podman’s behavior at all.

As for root, that’s worse… Ideally the whole sequence of fallbacks (or those parts of it that are actually necessary?) in `pkg/util.GetRuntimeDir` would be exactly the same as in `c/image/config`, shared in a subpackage somewhere. That’s not exactly `getPathToAuth`, though. Maybe a `c/image/pkg/docker/config/directory`?

One other awkward moment about all this is credential helper configuration — the approach we’ve been using now, to create/edit a config file in the first possible directory in the fallback sequence, is rather incorrect if a file later in the fallback sequence already exists and specifies credential helpers: creating the new file causes that credential helper configuration to be ignored. So, `GetRuntimeDir` really should be `GetDockerAuthFilePath` returning a path exactly to the file to create/modify (while Podman needs a runtime directory for other purposes, sure).

Just exporting `getPathToAuth` doesn’t look like the right API.
I am fine with containers/image owning this, and podman, buildah, skopeo should allow it to handle it, if possible.
